content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662 322-5660 322-5901 322-6441 322-6444

March 1, 1985


Stanley Sniff
P.O. Box 1414
La Quinta, CA  92253

Re: Your Request for Advice
    Our File No. A-85-018

Dear Mr. Sniff:

     We have received your letter concerning the La Quinta City
Council. As I explained in our telephone conversation, the Fair
Political Practices Commission issues specific advice to a
person only with respect to that person's duties under the
Political Reform Act. Furthermore, the Fair Political Practices
Commission does not have jurisdiction with regard to the Brown
Act or other statutory provisions not included in the Political
Reform Act. Therefore, I have not answered your questions about
the La Quinta City Council.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        Kathryn E. Donovan
                                        Counsel
                                        Legal Division

KED:plh

sections.question_synthetic: The requester asked for FPPC advice regarding matters related to the La Quinta City Council, including questions about the Brown Act and other statutory provisions.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide advice because it only issues guidance on matters under the Political Reform Act, not the Brown Act or other statutory provisions outside its jurisdiction.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter with no formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections. The letter explains jurisdictional limitations rather than providing substantive legal advice. Document contains only brief explanation of why advice cannot be provided.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The requester asked for FPPC advice regarding matters related to the La Quinta City Council, including questions about the Brown Act and other statutory provisions.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC declines to provide advice because it only issues guidance on matters under the Political Reform Act, not the Brown Act or other statutory provisions outside its jurisdiction.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declines to provide advice to Stanley Sniff regarding his questions about the La Quinta City Council because those questions fall outside the FPPC's jurisdiction under the Political Reform Act.
